sections.label: Workflows
sections.title: Workflows
paragraphs: ICOS workflows define how capture, reflection, review, and continuity move through the product system.
Each workflow is designed to preserve structure across sessions so the user can return to the same cognitive thread without losing context.
The workflow layer turns isolated interaction into a governed sequence of reusable product operations.

sections.label: Capture Flow
sections.title: Capture Flow
paragraphs: The capture flow receives voice, text, notes, questions, and structured inputs as product-grade material.
Captured material is prepared for interpretation, organization, and later retrieval inside the user profile.
This creates a consistent entry point for reflection, memory, and long-term continuity.

sections.label: Reflection Flow
sections.title: Reflection Flow
paragraphs: The reflection flow returns captured material through a structured response layer that helps the user inspect patterns, priorities, and recurring themes.
It supports deliberate review rather than passive conversation.
The system is designed to make each reflection more useful as prior context accumulates.

sections.label: Continuity Flow
sections.title: Continuity Flow
paragraphs: The continuity flow connects present interaction with earlier profile history and future review.
It gives ICOS the ability to preserve threads across time without treating every session as a blank start.
This makes the product more valuable as the user builds a longer record of use.

sections.label: Review Flow
sections.title: Review Flow
paragraphs: The review flow helps the user return to saved material, evaluate development, and refine earlier conclusions.
It supports a more disciplined relationship with memory and self-analysis.
Review becomes a product capability rather than a manual archive search.
